channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Why is Israel allowed to take part in the Eurovision song contest?
item.description: Five countries are boycotting the 70th edition of Eurovision on May 16 in protest over Israel's genocidal war on Gaza.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 74 as Tehran says ready for ‘aggression’
item.description: US-Iran tensions remain high as Tehran warns against 'aggression' and Trump says the ceasefire is on 'life support'.

item.title: Malaysia rescues 23 migrants after boat capsizes, 14 still missing
item.description: Authorities deploy boats, helicopter and surveillance aircraft to search for missing migrants off Pangkor Island.

item.title: Thunder outlasts Lakers in Game 4 to complete NBA playoff sweep
item.description: Reigning champions Oklahoma City Thunder remain undefeated in playoffs and move on to Western Conference finals.

item.title: Global energy crisis highlights meagre oil buffers in developing world
item.description: Governments in the Global South are among the least prepared to respond to the oil shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: US moves to release more oil stockpiles under IEA agreement
item.description: US Department of Energy moves to transfer 53.3 million barrels amid rising oil prices.

item.title: Israel approves law on public trials, death penalty for October 7 detainees
item.description: Rights groups warn that the bill makes the death penalty easier to impose and strips fair trial protections.

item.title: Hundreds more displaced as gang violence escalates in Haiti’s capital
item.description: Renewed clashes between rival gangs in Port-au-Prince have forced hundreds to flee their homes.

item.title: Flotilla activist Thiago Ávila arrives in Brazil
item.description: Brazilian activist Thiago Ávila returned to São Paulo after being detained and deported from Israel.

item.title: California ex-mayor admits acting as agent of China, US authorities say
item.description: Ex-mayor of wealthy Los Angeles suburb promoted pro-China propaganda at behest of Chinese officials, prosecutors say.

item.title: Lebanese in south refuse to flee again despite escalating Israeli strikes
item.description: Obaida Hitto reports from southern Lebanon, where residents say they will not leave again despite intensifying strikes.

item.title: Bolivia issues warrant for Evo Morales’s arrest after court no-show
item.description: The ex-Bolivian president is on trial for allegedly fathering a child with a 15-year-old girl while in office.

item.title: Last passengers from hantavirus-hit ship evacuated; American tests positive
item.description: US officials say 18 Americans are being monitored for symptoms, as WHO insists risk to public remains low.

item.title: Syria restores credit card payments in effort to rejoin the global economy
item.description: Syria restores credit card payments in effort to rejoin the global economy.

item.title: Roads blocked in Bolivia as protesters demand president’s resignation
item.description: Bolivia faces growing unrest as widespread road blockades disrupt travel across major cities.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump slams Iranian proposal as ceasefire hangs by a thread
item.description: Iran's powerful parliamentary speaker says US has 'no alternative but to accept' Tehran's 14-point proposal to end war.

item.title: Haiti’s PM casts doubt on presidential vote by August as gang clashes grow
item.description: Alix Didier ⁠Fils-Aime says Haiti is too unstable for elections as a new wave of violence forces hospital evacuations.

item.title: Trump says he will suspend petrol tax amid soaring US fuel prices
item.description: Suspending a federal tax requires an act of Congress, and there may be procedural delays before it can be achieved.

item.title: EU, UK sanction Russians over deportations of Ukrainian children
item.description: Russian institutions and officials accused of systemic deportation and indoctrination of children during war on Ukraine.

item.title: Lamine Yamal waves Palestine flag as Barcelona celebrate La Liga win
item.description: Tens of thousands of Barca fans pack streets of Spanish city during open-top bus parade after football league victory.

item.title: EU sanctions violent Israeli settlers after months of deadlock
item.description: The assets of violent Israeli settlers and settler organisations are being targeted in a new EU sanctions package.

item.title: Barcelona player Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag at parade
item.description: Barcelona player Lamine Yamal unfurled a Palestinian flag as the team paraded through the streets of Barcelona.

item.title: Rebranding Francafrique
item.description: France is trying to rebrand itself during a summit with African heads of states in Kenya.

item.title: Palestinians mark 78 years since Nakba amid ongoing displacement
item.description: 78 years of memory: Palestinians rally in Khan Yunis to mark the Nakba.

item.title: Could the latest violence in DR Congo undermine truce efforts?
item.description: Lesser-known armed groups have carried out a spate of attacks in the country's northeast.
